message:
fix(acfs target_home resolution): prefer NSS/getent over inherited TARGET_HOME; propagate full ACFS_* env set through sudo/run_as_target wrappers

Two coupled refactors to close repeated "why is TARGET_HOME wrong?"
footguns we hit across install.sh, acfs-global, acfs-update, the
smoke/status libs, and the ACFS manifest bootstrap snippets. Plus the
generator and its generated artifacts.

1. Prefer passwd-resolved target_home over inherited TARGET_HOME
--------------------------------------------------------------
Previously, most call sites checked `TARGET_HOME` first and only fell
back to `getent passwd` / `passwd_home_from_entry` if TARGET_HOME was
empty. That meant a stale TARGET_HOME inherited from a prior
`sudo -E` invocation would override the actual home directory of the
target user — we saw that manifest target_home wind up as /root when
running as ubuntu, or lose the trailing-slash normalization.

Flip the order everywhere to:

   1. Try `acfs_generated_getent_passwd_entry` +
      `acfs_generated_passwd_home_from_entry` first.
   2. Fall back to /root when TARGET_USER == "root".
   3. Fall back to `$HOME` when the current user matches TARGET_USER
      (requires `$HOME` to be an absolute, non-"/" path).
   4. Only then honour an inherited `TARGET_HOME` — and strip any
      trailing slash.
   5. Error out if nothing resolved; never silently use "/" or empty.

Applies to:

- `install.sh::init_target_paths` — direct rewrite.
- `packages/manifest/src/generate.ts` — `_ACFS_RESOLVED_TARGET_HOME`
  scratch variable replaces the conditional-inside-conditional ladder
  so the emitted `.sh` artifacts are easier to audit. Emits 222 lines
  of updated generated content.
- `acfs.manifest.yaml` — updates the inline bootstrap snippets in the
  `base` and `projects` modules to match the new resolution order.
- `scripts/lib/smoke_test.sh`, `status.sh` — `_smoke_initial_current_home`
  / `_status_initial_current_home` now try the live resolver before
  falling back to the cached "sourced from" value, and the
  TARGET_HOME ladder is re-ordered to match.
- `scripts/lib/user.sh`, `install_helpers.sh`, `doctor.sh`,
  `export-config.sh`, `info.sh`, `changelog.sh`, `cheatsheet.sh`,
  `continue.sh`, `dashboard.sh`, `nightly_update.sh`, `support.sh`,
  `services-setup.sh` — small targeted fixes to any place that
  consulted TARGET_HOME before the resolver.

Generator tests in `packages/manifest/src/generate.test.ts` assert the
new ordering for both the base and projects modules, and cover the
trailing-slash strip case.

Bats tests in `tests/unit/lib/test_install_helpers.bats`,
`test_update.bats`, and `test_user.bats` cover the real-shell side of
the same contract, including the "inherited TARGET_HOME is stale,
passwd entry wins" case and the "passwd entry missing, TARGET_HOME
fallback wins" case.

2. Propagate the full ACFS_* env set through sudo / run_as_target
---------------------------------------------------------------
`run_as_target` in install.sh and its clones in
`scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh::run_as_target` /
`run_as_root_shell` previously only passed a small subset of
ACFS_* vars. Downstream snippets that rely on
`ACFS_LIB_DIR` / `ACFS_GENERATED_DIR` / `ACFS_ASSETS_DIR` /
`ACFS_CHECKSUMS_YAML` / `ACFS_MANIFEST_YAML` / `CHECKSUMS_FILE`
were getting undefined values when invoked through sudo, forcing
re-computation from relative paths that were already wrong by then.

Widen the env_args preamble in all three functions to include the
full ACFS_* + CHECKSUMS_FILE set + ACFS_REF. `install.sh` also
defaults `CHECKSUMS_FILE` from an inherited
`${CHECKSUMS_FILE:-}` if `ACFS_CHECKSUMS_YAML` is unset, so
sub-processes invoked by the bootstrap see a consistent value.

scripts/acfs-global + scripts/acfs-update
`exec_as_target_user` and the analogous main() branch in
acfs-global now export `HOME=` alongside `TARGET_HOME=` so interactive
subshells (bash/zsh rc files) see the resolved target home instead of
the root HOME inherited through sudo.

Regenerated artifacts
---------------------
All files under `scripts/generated/` are deterministic outputs of
`packages/manifest/src/generate.ts`. Regenerated via
`cd packages/manifest && bun run generate` after the source edits, so
ACFS_MANIFEST_SHA256 + internal checksums match — this is the
documented drift guard (see AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md).

This change also supersedes the recent upstream auto-fix commit
`58606ed5 fix(manifest): auto-fix generated artifact checksum drift`:
the drift-fix bot regenerated from the old generator; the new
regeneration produces the larger, correct output from the updated
generator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+@test "services-setup: init_target_context repairs stale TARGET_HOME from trusted passwd data" {
+    local services_setup="$PROJECT_ROOT/scripts/services-setup.sh"
+    local test_current_user
+    local test_trusted_home
+    local test_stale_home
+    local stale_bun
+    local trusted_bun

+
+    test_current_user="$(command id -un 2>/dev/null || command whoami 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    test_trusted_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+    test_stale_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+    stale_bun="$test_stale_home/.local/bin/bun"
+    trusted_bun="$test_trusted_home/.local/bin/bun"
+    mkdir -p "$test_trusted_home/.local/bin" "$test_stale_home/.local/bin"
+    touch "$stale_bun" "$trusted_bun"
+    chmod +x "$stale_bun" "$trusted_bun"
+
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^services_setup_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^services_setup_valid_target_user()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^services_setup_validate_target_user()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^services_setup_passwd_home_from_entry()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^resolve_home_dir()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^services_setup_validate_bin_dir_for_home()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^find_user_bin()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^init_target_context()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^run_as_user()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+
+    log_error() {
+        printf '%s\n' "$*" >&2
+    }
+
+    services_setup_resolve_current_user() {
+        printf '%s\n' "$test_current_user"
+    }
+
+    services_setup_getent_passwd_entry() {
+        if [[ -z "${1:-}" ]]; then
+            printf '%s:x:1000:1000::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$test_current_user" "$test_trusted_home"
+            printf 'stale-user:x:1001:1001::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$test_stale_home"
+            return 0
+        fi
+        if [[ "${1:-}" == "$test_current_user" ]]; then
+            printf '%s:x:1000:1000::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$test_current_user" "$test_trusted_home"
+            return 0
+        fi
+        return 1
+    }
+
+    export TARGET_USER="$test_current_user"
+    export TARGET_HOME="$test_stale_home"
+    export HOME="$test_stale_home"
+    export ACFS_BIN_DIR="$test_stale_home/.local/bin"
+    export BUN_BIN="$stale_bun"
+
+    init_target_context
+
+    [[ "$TARGET_HOME" == "$test_trusted_home" ]] || {
+        printf 'TARGET_HOME was not repaired: %s\n' "$TARGET_HOME" >&2
+        return 1
+    }
+    [[ "$ACFS_BIN_DIR" != "$test_stale_home/.local/bin" ]] || {
+        printf 'ACFS_BIN_DIR still points at stale home\n' >&2
+        return 1
+    }
+    [[ "$BUN_BIN" == "$trusted_bun" ]] || {
+        printf 'BUN_BIN was not repaired: %s\n' "$BUN_BIN" >&2
+        return 1
+    }
+
+    env_home_output="$(run_as_user bash -c 'printf "%s\n" "$HOME"')"
+    [[ "$env_home_output" == "$test_trusted_home" ]] || {
+        printf 'run_as_user HOME was not repaired: %s\n' "$env_home_output" >&2
+        return 1
+    }
+}

+@test "read-only context helpers repair stale TARGET_HOME for current target user" {
+    local current_user
+    local current_home
+    local stale_home
+    local label
+    local script
+    local prepare_cmd
+    local target_var
+    local failures=""
+
+    current_user="$(command id -un 2>/dev/null || command whoami 2>/dev/null || true)"

+    [[ -n "$current_user" && -n "$current_home" ]] || skip "Could not resolve current user home"

+        [[ -n "$label" ]] || continue

+            TARGET_USER="$current_user" \
+            TARGET_HOME="$stale_home" \
+            HOME="$stale_home" \
+            PATH="/usr/bin:/bin" \
+            bash -c '
+                set -euo pipefail
+                source "$1"
+                eval "$2"
+                printf "%s\n" "${!3}"
+            ' _ "$script" "$prepare_cmd" "$target_var"
+
+        if [[ "$status" -ne 0 || "$output" != "$current_home" ]]; then
+            printf -v failures '%s%s: status=%s output=%s\n' "$failures" "$label" "$status" "$output"
+        fi
+    done <<EOF

+info|$PROJECT_ROOT/scripts/lib/info.sh|info_prepare_context|TARGET_HOME

+smoke|$PROJECT_ROOT/scripts/lib/smoke_test.sh|:|_SMOKE_TARGET_HOME
+EOF
+
+    if [[ -n "$failures" ]]; then
+        printf '%s' "$failures" >&2
+        return 1
+    fi
+}
